title: thelesperma gracile
intro: Thelesperma gracile, also known as Prairie Silverbell or Gray Hill muhly, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Native American medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Thelesperma gracile is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Thelesperma gracile can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
